examples.title: Complex Number Addition with Operator Overloading
examples.description: Demonstrates binary operator overloading for Complex number addition
examples.explanation: Shows both member function (operator+) and friend function (operator<<) overloading. The + operator adds real and imaginary parts separately. Stream operator allows easy output.

examples.title: Prefix and Postfix Increment Operators
examples.description: Demonstrates the difference between prefix and postfix operator overloading
examples.explanation: Prefix ++ increments first, returns reference to this. Postfix ++ returns a copy of the object before increment. The dummy int parameter distinguishes postfix from prefix.

examples.title: Assignment Operator with Self-Assignment Check
examples.description: Demonstrates proper implementation of assignment operator
examples.explanation: Shows proper assignment operator with: 1) Self-assignment check (this != &s), 2) Deep copy (new memory allocation), 3) Return by reference for chaining, 4) Works with Rule of Three.

examples.title: Subscript Operator Overloading
examples.explanation: Shows two versions of operator[]: const and non-const. Non-const returns reference for modification. Const version returns const reference for read-only access.

examples.title: Type Conversion Operators
examples.description: Demonstrates conversion from user-defined type to built-in type
examples.explanation: Shows conversion operators that convert class object to other types. Defined without return type. Enables implicit conversion (use 'explicit' to prevent).

examples.title: Function Call Operator (Functor)
examples.description: Demonstrates overloading () to create function objects
examples.explanation: Demonstrates functor/function object. Class with overloaded operator() can be used like a function but maintains state (increment value). Used extensively in STL algorithms.
